mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 4.1% as of Dec 2024 with payrolls at 159536k
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months with positive PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates above 4% and declining industrial production induces monetary restraint that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations simultaneously, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.48% and INDPRO vintage at 101.9621 as of Nov 2024
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) versus single-proxy models over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production predict negative S&P 500 forward returns because operating leverage reverses and margin compression amplifies negative earnings surprises as capacity utilization falls.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down to 99.2925 in Nov 2024 from prior estimates
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when INDPRO declines month-over-month while UNRATE is below 5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales exceeding 0.5% signals stronger-than-expected consumer demand that drives near-term earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above a 3.5% year-over-year threshold sustain elevated equity risk premiums by forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which depresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 317.685 in Dec 2024 implying YoY inflation above 3.5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and falling nonfarm payrolls constitute a joint labor market contraction signal that reduces aggregate income and consumption faster than either variable alone, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and PAYEMS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-occurring months, or a logistic regression using both proxies shows no significant interaction effect (p > 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% annualized confirms durable aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) following quarters with GDPC1 growth greater than 2.0% over any 5-year rolling window.
